title: Lithocarpus bennettii
intro: Lithocarpus bennettii, commonly known as the Himalayan Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus bennettii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, terpenoids, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lithocarpus bennettii is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to make various herbal preparations, including capsules, tablets, and teas.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus bennettii is classified within the family Fagaceae. It is a genus of deciduous or evergreen trees native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. The classification of Lithocarpus bennettii places it within the subfamily Quercoideae, which is a group of Fagaceae species that have similar characteristics to the genus Quercus. This classification is based on the tree's morphology, leaf structure, and reproductive features. Further study and analysis may be necessary to refine the classification of Lithocarpus bennettii.

constituents: Lithocarpus bennettii contains active constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
